If your team needs uninterrupted high-volume reviews, an organization admin can enable usage-based reviews. > >

> How do review limits work? > > CodeRabbit enforces per-developer PR review limits for each organization. Most developers receive the normal plan review availability. > > For paid Pro and Pro+ PR reviews, CodeRabbit uses adaptive limits for sustained high-volume activity. When a developer's recent PR review activity reaches the 95th percentile or higher among CodeRabbit users, additional reviews become available more gradually as earlier reviews age out of the rolling window. > > Please refer [docs](https://docs.coderabbit.ai/management/plans#rate-limits) for additional details. > >

✨ Finishing Touches
🧪 Generate unit tests (beta) - [ ] Create PR with unit tests - [ ] Commit unit tests in branch `themeable`
--- Comment `@coderabbitai help` to get the list of available commands.

## Code Review

This pull request introduces a theme system to the card deck generator, allowing reskins of the classic deck (such as the newly added Chicago theme) with custom card names and color-palette overlays. It moves the default cards to themes/classic/cards.yaml, adds theme resolution and token deep-merging in src/data/themes.py, updates the CLI and rendering pipeline to support themes, and expands the test suite to validate theme invariants. The review feedback suggests improving security and robustness by validating theme names against available_themes() to prevent path traversal, specifying explicit UTF-8 encoding when opening files, and dynamically parametrizing SVG validation tests.

> [!IMPORTANT]
> The [consumer version of Gemini Code Assist on GitHub](https://developers.google.com/gemini-code-assist/docs/review-repo-code) is being sunset. Starting **June 18, 2026**, new organization installations will be blocked, and all code review activity will officially cease on **July 17, 2026**.
> For more details on the timeline and next steps, please review the [Help Documentation](https://developers.google.com/gemini-code-assist/docs/deprecations/consumer-code-review).

![security-high](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/security-high-priority.svg) ![high](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/high-priority.svg)

To prevent potential path traversal vulnerabilities (e.g., passing `../../` as a theme name) and to ensure consistent validation, we should validate that the requested theme `name` is strictly one of the available themes. Checking `name not in available_themes()` is a robust and clean way to achieve this.

```suggestion
def theme_cards_path(name: str) -> Path:
    """Path to a theme's cards.yaml, validating the theme exists."""
    if name not in available_themes():
        raise ValueError(
            f"unknown theme {name!r}; available: {', '.join(available_themes())}"
        )
    return THEMES_DIR / name / "cards.yaml"
```

![security-high](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/security-high-priority.svg) ![high](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/high-priority.svg)
